2603.28295.title: Evaluating LLMs for Answering Student Questions in Introductory Programming Courses
2603.28295.paper_summary: The rapid emergence of Large Language Models (LLMs) presents both opportunities and challenges for programming education. While students increasingly use generative AI tools, direct access often hinders the learning process by providing complete solutions rather than pedagogical hints. Concurrently, educators face significant workload and scalability challenges when providing timely, personalized feedback. This study investigates the capabilities of LLMs to safely and effectively assist educators in answering student questions within a CS1 programming course. To achieve this, we established a rigorous, reproducible evaluation process by curating a benchmark dataset of 170 authentic student questions from a learning management system, paired with ground-truth responses authored by subject matter experts. Because traditional text-matching metrics are insufficient for evaluating open-ended educational responses, we developed and validated a custom LLM-as-a-Judge metric optimized for assessing pedagogical accuracy. Our findings demonstrate that models, such as Gemini 3 flash, can surpass the quality baseline of typical educator responses, achieving high alignment with expert pedagogical standards. To mitigate persistent risks like hallucination and ensure alignment with course-specific context, we advocate for a "teacher-in-the-loop" implementation. Finally, we abstract our methodology into a task-agnostic evaluation framework, advocating for a shift in the development of educational LLM tools from ad-hoc, post-deployment testing to a quantifiable, pre-deployment validation process.
2603.28295.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）的快速出現為程式設計教育帶來了機會與挑戰。<lb/>雖然學生們越來越多地使用生成式AI工具，但直接訪問往往通過提供完整的解決方案而非教學提示來阻礙學習過程。<lb/>同時，教育工作者在提供及時、個性化的反饋時面臨著重大的工作負擔和可擴展性挑戰。<lb/>本研究探討了LLMs在CS1程式設計課程中安全有效地協助教育者回答學生問題的能力。<lb/>為了實現這一目標，我們通過策劃一個包含170個來自學習管理系統的真實學生問題的基準數據集，並配以由主題專家撰寫的真實回應，建立了一個嚴謹、可重複的評估過程。<lb/>由於傳統的文本匹配指標不足以評估開放式教育回應，我們開發並驗證了一個自定義的LLM作為評判者指標，旨在評估教學準確性。<lb/>我們的研究結果顯示，像Gemini 3 flash這樣的模型可以超越典型教育者回應的質量基準，並與專家教學標準達成高度一致。<lb/>為了減輕持續存在的風險，如幻覺，並確保與課程特定上下文的一致性，我們倡導實施“教師在環路中”的方法。<lb/>最後，我們將我們的方法論抽象為一個任務無關的評估框架，主張將教育LLM工具的開發從臨時的、部署後的測試轉變為可量化的、部署前的驗證過程。
2603.28295.author: Thomas Van Mullem et.al.
2603.28295.authors: Thomas Van Mullem, Bart Mesuere, Peter Dawyndt
